title: Final SDK envelope persistence path audit (verification-first)
intent: Audit every live venue submit path in src/execution/executor.py to confirm the SDK-returned final envelope/order payload is persisted via _persist_final_submission_envelope_payload (executor.py:432) at every contact site (entry submit, exit submit, rejected submit, FOK/FAK filled response, open-order ACK, SUBMIT_UNKNOWN_SIDE_EFFECT). Implement persistence ONLY at sites the audit proves are missing it; do not opportunistically refactor confirmed-OK sites.

asserted_invariants.text: docs/operations/task_2026-05-04_zeus_may3_review_remediation/phases/T1G/audit/sdk_envelope_path_audit.md exists, enumerates every live SDK contact site in src/execution/executor.py (and src/venue/polymarket_v2_adapter.py), classifies each as VERIFIED_PERSISTS / NEEDS_FIX / NOT_LIVE_PATH, and cites file:line for each verdict. The audit covers at minimum: entry submit (executor.py:1609), exit submit (executor.py:2291), SUBMIT_REJECTED (executor.py:1495,1662,1694,2099,2138,2169,2342,2373), SUBMIT_UNKNOWN_SIDE_EFFECT (executor.py:1568,2251), and the venue-adapter post_order paths (polymarket_v2_adapter.py:345-369).

tests/test_final_sdk_envelope_persistence.py contains test_every_live_submit_persists_final_sdk_envelope, test_rejected_submit_persists_reject_payload, and test_exit_submit_persists_final_envelope. Each test exercises the relevant code path with a fake SDK and asserts that venue_command_repo.insert_submission_envelope (or the equivalent post-T1G persistence helper) was called with the SDK-returned payload, NOT just the pre-submit envelope. The tests fail before T1G when run against any path the audit classifies as NEEDS_FIX.

If the audit finds the existing _persist_final_submission_envelope_payload helper at executor.py:432 is correctly called at all live contact sites, T1G's source-edit scope is empty and T1G closes with audit + tests only. If the audit finds gaps, the executor adds source-edit work to files_touched in a phase-amendment commit before patching; the phase MUST NOT silently widen scope.

T1G does NOT introduce new SDK call surfaces. It only audits and adds persistence at existing call sites. polymarket_v2_adapter.py:312 submit() is owned by T1F; T1G must not duplicate or regress T1F's live-bound assertion.

round2_grep_verification.audit_target_per_master_plan: MASTER_PLAN_v2 §10 row T1G enumerates: entry submit, exit submit, compatibility submit, rejected submit, FOK/FAK filled response, open-order ACK. Compatibility submit is owned by T1F; T1G inherits T1F's live-bound assertion as the gate that prevents it from reaching SDK. FOK/FAK is not a separate code path — it is selected via envelope.order_type ('FOK'/'FAK') consumed by polymarket_v2_adapter.py:347 client.create_and_post_order; T1G audits that FOK/FAK responses also flow through _persist_final_submission_envelope_payload, with explicit test fixture coverage.

_planner_notes.loc_estimate_basis: T1G is verification-first. Audit document: ~80 lines. New test file: ~250 lines (one test per audited site + fakes for SDK responses). Source patches if needed: estimated 0-100 lines depending on audit verdicts. Total 250 LOC base + up to 100 LOC contingent on audit. The phase.files_touched lists ONLY the audit + test file; if audit finds NEEDS_FIX gaps, executor opens a phase amendment to add src/execution/executor.py and/or src/state/venue_command_repo.py.
_planner_notes.expected_artifact_override: Per ORCHESTRATOR_RUNBOOK §5 and ORCHESTRATOR_RUNBOOK §10, expected_artifact is 'execution/execution_result.md' by default. T1G overrides this to 'audit/sdk_envelope_path_audit.md' because the phase is verification-first (per MASTER_PLAN_v2 §10 row T1G 'Verification-first: trace entry submit, exit submit ...'). The execution_result.md is still produced if any source edits land, but the audit document is the primary phase deliverable.
_planner_notes.topology_admission: BLOCKED at planner time — generic profile does not admit src/execution/executor.py + src/state/venue_command_repo.py for write-intent edit if the audit finds gaps. Resolution per LOCK_DECISION Amendment 4. Audit-only mode (read + new test + new audit doc) MAY be admissible under a different profile route; planner did not run that variant.
_planner_notes.planning_lock_required: true if source edits are needed; false if audit-only outcome
_planner_notes.k0_k1_classification: K0 LIVE BOUNDARY (executor venue submit). Critic + security-reviewer-opus required per ORCHESTRATOR_RUNBOOK §12 T1G row.
coordinator_blockers: T1F closeout MUST land first — T1G inherits the live-bound assertion as the gate for compatibility-submit attempts.
_planner_notes.predecessor_dependency: T1F (live-bound assertion) MUST land first. T1F covers compat submit blocking; T1G covers final envelope persistence at all admitted live paths. Without T1F, the audit cannot rely on placeholder envelopes being blocked, contaminating the test fixtures.
_planner_notes.closeout_drift_resolution: T2F-DOC-DRIFT-CLEANED: typed counter sink landed in T2F B1 (2026-05-05). All T1 phases share src/observability/counters.py. No per-phase DDL or schema drift outstanding.
